name: Brushware
classification_notes: Listed under Script classification by ITF; the name and category suggest a brush-drawn script typeface typical of hand-lettered display work.
summary: A single-weight brush script from Indian Type Foundry intended for display and hand-lettered visual contexts.
description: A brush script offered as a single Regular weight, positioned within ITF's script category alongside other expressive and hand-drawn faces. With only one style in the family, it functions primarily as a display face for headlines, packaging and short-form copy that calls for an informal, gestural quality. Related fonts on the ITF site include Bitscript and Blonde Script, pointing to a consistent interest in expressive Latin script faces.
foundry_blurb: ITF classify Brushware as a Script font, sold as a single-font complete family at £59.70.

name: Bitscript
classification_notes: ITF classifies this as a Script font; the name and related fonts suggest a brush or informal handwritten style typical of display script typefaces.
summary: A single-weight script typeface from Indian Type Foundry, positioned alongside brush and handwritten display faces in their catalogue.
description: Available in one weight, Bitscript sits in ITF's script category alongside brush-style faces such as Brushware. With a single Regular style, it is suited to display and short-form use where an informal handwritten character is needed. The limited weight range keeps it focused as a display tool rather than a workhorse text family.
foundry_blurb: ITF classify Bitscript as a Script font, offered as a single-font family at one weight.

name: Nusrat
classification_notes: ITF classifies Nusrat under the Script category; the page offers no further subcategory detail, but its grouping alongside brush and pen scripts suggests a calligraphic or handwritten style.
summary: A single-weight script from Indian Type Foundry, sitting within their broader collection of calligraphic and handwritten display faces.
description: Nusrat is a script typeface offered as a single Regular style, priced within ITF's mid-range script offerings. The page provides limited technical detail, but its placement alongside brush and pen scripts such as Brushware and Spencerio suggests a handwritten or calligraphic character suited to display and short-form typesetting. Only Latin script coverage is indicated.
foundry_blurb: Indian Type Foundry list Nusrat under their Script classification, offering it as a single font at 74.62 GBP, positioned among a range of calligraphic and handwritten typefaces.

name: Kohinoor Zerone
classification_notes: Part of the wider Kohinoor family from ITF, Zerone is a Latin sans-serif in the humanist tradition, offered in two styles (Zero and One) as a free release.
summary: A free two-style Latin sans from Indian Type Foundry, sitting within the broader multilingual Kohinoor family.
description: Kohinoor Zerone is offered as a free download in two styles, Zero and One, forming a compact companion to ITF's expansive Kohinoor family which spans Devanagari, Tamil, Arabic and numerous other scripts. The family is designed with screen and text use in mind, consistent with the broader Kohinoor range. Its availability as a free release makes it accessible as an entry point to ITF's type ecosystem.
foundry_blurb: Indian Type Foundry offer Kohinoor Zerone as a free two-font family, comprising the Zero and One styles, as part of the wider Kohinoor type family available across multiple scripts and writing systems.

name: Duplet Rounded
classification_notes: A rounded variant of the Duplet family, characterised by softened terminals on an otherwise clean geometric or humanist sans structure, placing it firmly in the rounded sans subcategory.
summary: A rounded companion to ITF's Duplet family, offered in seven weights with matching italics for a total of fourteen styles.
description: Part of the broader Duplet family alongside Duplet and Duplet Open, the Rounded cut applies softened terminals throughout, lending a gentler character suited to UI, branding and editorial contexts where warmth is preferred over sharpness. Seven weights run from Thin to Extrabold, each paired with an italic. The close relationship to its sibling families allows for mixing across the Duplet range.
foundry_blurb: Duplet Rounded is a 14-font family available from Indian Type Foundry, sitting alongside Duplet and Duplet Open as part of the wider Duplet superfamily. The complete package is available at a bundle price.

name: Duplet
classification_notes: Duplet presents clean, geometric letterforms typical of a modern geometric sans-serif, with a structured design that extends across a broad weight range and related rounded and open subfamilies.
summary: A geometric sans-serif from Indian Type Foundry spanning seven weights with italics, accompanied by Rounded and Open subfamily variants.
description: Duplet offers a clean geometric construction across seven weights from Thin to Extrabold, each with a matching italic, giving designers 14 styles in the base family. Two companion families, Duplet Rounded and Duplet Open, extend the system for softer or more open-aperture applications. The range suits interface, editorial and branding work where a disciplined but friendly geometric sans is needed.
foundry_blurb: Indian Type Foundry offer Duplet as a complete 14-font family with related Rounded and Open variants, each available separately or as part of the full system, priced per style or as a complete package.

name: Duplet Open
classification_notes: Duplet Open belongs to the geometric sans tradition, sharing its structural DNA with the Duplet family; the 'Open' variant suggests open apertures that distinguish it from the standard cut.
summary: A geometric sans with open apertures, offered in seven weights with matching italics as part of ITF's broader Duplet family.
description: Part of Indian Type Foundry's Duplet family alongside the standard and Rounded cuts, Duplet Open distinguishes itself through open apertures that improve legibility at smaller sizes. The family runs from Thin to Extrabold across fourteen styles with full italic coverage. It suits interface, editorial and corporate work where a clean geometric structure is needed without the closed forms of a strict geometric sans.
foundry_blurb: Duplet Open is part of ITF's Duplet family, available in 14 fonts across seven weights from Thin to Extrabold, each with a matching italic. It is sold alongside Duplet and Duplet Rounded as related subfamilies.

name: Snooze
classification_notes: Snooze is categorised as a script-style display face by ITF, suited to decorative and headline use rather than extended reading.
summary: A three-weight display script from Indian Type Foundry, spanning Light to Bold for headline and decorative work.
description: Snooze offers three weights from Light through Regular to Bold, giving designers a modest range for logotype, packaging and editorial headline use. The family is compact, with no italics or condensed cuts, keeping its focus on decorative impact rather than versatility. At under £75 for the complete family, it sits as an accessible option for casual display work.
foundry_blurb: ITF present Snooze as a script-classification display font available in three styles: Light, Regular and Bold, sold individually or as a complete family of three fonts.

name: Malhar
classification_notes: Classified as Script by ITF; the page lists it under the Script category with no further subdivision, suggesting a calligraphic or brush-style Latin script face.
summary: A five-weight script family from Indian Type Foundry, spanning Thin to Bold for display and editorial applications.
description: Malhar offers an unusually wide weight range for a script typeface, running from Thin through to Bold across five cuts. Released by Indian Type Foundry, it sits alongside other script offerings in their catalogue such as Katana and Blonde Script. The range of weights gives it flexibility across headline, packaging and branding contexts where tonal variation is needed.
foundry_blurb: ITF list Malhar as a Script classification font, available as a complete family of five fonts covering Thin, Light, Regular, Medium and Bold weights, sold individually or as a complete package.

name: Rustic
classification_notes: The name and weight range suggest a sturdy, characterful display typeface; the page classifies it under Script and display usage categories, pointing to an expressive, headline-oriented design.
summary: A five-weight display family from Indian Type Foundry, ranging from Regular to Heavy for headline and poster applications.
description: Five upright weights run from Regular through to Heavy, giving designers a compact but practical range for display settings. The family carries no italic or condensed cuts, keeping the focus squarely on weight contrast for headlines and short text. Priced individually or as a complete set of five fonts.
foundry_blurb: Indian Type Foundry offer Rustic as a complete family of five fonts spanning Regular to Heavy, available individually or as a bundled set, aimed at display and script-adjacent usage contexts.

name: Bombay Mono
classification_notes: Listed explicitly under the Mono classification by Indian Type Foundry; the name and single-style offering confirm a monospaced design with no variable or proportional cuts.
summary: A single-weight monospaced typeface from Indian Type Foundry, offered as a standalone font for code or technical use.
description: A monospaced design sold as a single font, positioned within ITF's broader catalogue alongside related families such as Monorama. The page offers little detail on its design origins or intended applications, but its classification and pricing place it in the professional mono segment. Related family Monorama suggests a broader mono design interest at the foundry.
foundry_blurb: Indian Type Foundry list Bombay Mono as a single-font monospaced release, available as a complete package for a one-off licence fee, with trial fonts available before purchase.

name: Schmalfette
classification_notes: Schmalfette ('schmal' meaning narrow in German, 'fette' meaning bold/fat) is a condensed, bold display typeface drawing on blackletter and heavy display traditions, designed for large-scale headline use.
summary: A single-weight condensed display face from ITF that draws on German blackletter and heavy condensed poster traditions.
description: The name references German typographic terminology — 'schmal' for narrow, 'fette' for bold — signalling its roots in condensed, heavyweight display lettering. Available in one Regular style, it is aimed squarely at headline and poster applications where vertical economy and visual impact are required. The family is compact at a single font, making it a focused, purpose-built choice rather than a versatile text workhorse.
foundry_blurb: Indian Type Foundry offer Schmalfette as a single-font family in Regular, priced at 37.31 GBP, positioned as a condensed display typeface suited to bold typographic applications.

name: Bookmark
classification_notes: The page lists Bookmark under a serif classification with a formal weight range from Light to Bold, suggesting a text-oriented transitional or contemporary serif design typical of ITF's Latin offerings.
summary: A four-weight Latin serif from Indian Type Foundry, covering Light through Bold for text and editorial use.
description: Offered in four weights from Light to Bold, Bookmark is a compact serif family suited to running text and publication work. The range is tight but covers the most common editorial requirements without extended width or italic variants. Indian Type Foundry position it alongside other text serifs in their Latin catalogue such as Begum and Tara.
foundry_blurb: Bookmark is a serif typeface available in four styles — Light, Regular, Medium and Bold — sold individually or as a complete family of four fonts.

name: Erika Hand
classification_notes: Classified as a script by ITF; the name and visual style indicate a casual handwritten typeface, consistent with the script category.
summary: A single-weight handwritten script from Indian Type Foundry, designed for informal and casual typographic uses.
description: A casual handwritten script offered in one weight, positioned for uses where a personal, hand-drawn quality is needed. The family contains just one font, keeping its scope narrow and its application focused. Suited to packaging, greeting cards and similar contexts where a natural pen-written feel is appropriate.
foundry_blurb: ITF classify Erika Hand as a script typeface, available as a single Regular weight. The complete family is sold as one font.

name: Picket
classification_notes: Picket presents clean, upright forms with no italic variants and a geometric construction, suggesting a geometric sans-serif classification. The even weight distribution across the six styles reinforces this reading.
summary: A six-weight geometric sans from Indian Type Foundry, running from Extralight to Bold without italic companions.
description: Six upright weights from Extralight through to Bold make up this geometric sans from Indian Type Foundry. With no italics or condensed cuts, the family is compact but covers a practical range for text and display work. The even progression across weights gives it flexibility for hierarchical typesetting.
foundry_blurb: Picket is available as a complete family of six fonts from Extralight to Bold, sold individually or as a bundle through Indian Type Foundry.

name: Kohinoor Malayalam
classification_notes: Kohinoor Malayalam is a humanist sans-serif designed for the Malayalam script, part of a large multi-script Kohinoor family spanning Indian and Arabic writing systems. The letterforms follow the organic curves of the Malayalam script while maintaining consistency with the broader Kohinoor family aesthetic.
summary: A five-weight sans-serif for the Malayalam script, part of ITF's extensive Kohinoor multi-script family spanning Latin, Devanagari, Arabic and numerous Indic scripts.
description: Part of Indian Type Foundry's ambitious Kohinoor project, this Malayalam companion covers five weights from Light to Bold without italics, following the conventions of the script. It sits alongside siblings for Kannada, Telugu, Bangla, Gujarati, Gurmukhi, Devanagari, Tamil and Arabic, making it a practical choice for multilingual South Asian publishing and interface work. The weight range gives sufficient flexibility for body text and headline use within Malayalam-language contexts.
foundry_blurb: Kohinoor Malayalam is one of many script-specific members of the Kohinoor family from Indian Type Foundry, offered in five styles from Light to Bold. The broader Kohinoor project covers a wide range of scripts including Latin, Devanagari, Arabic and multiple South and North Indian writing systems.

name: Kohinoor Kannada
classification_notes: Part of the broader Kohinoor multi-script family, Kohinoor Kannada is a humanist sans-serif designed for the Kannada script, following the design conventions of the wider Kohinoor system which balances legibility with clean, contemporary forms.
summary: The Kannada branch of ITF's wide-ranging Kohinoor family, offering five weights for text use in the Kannada script.
description: One of many script-specific members of the Kohinoor family from Indian Type Foundry, this release covers the Kannada script in five weights from Light to Bold. The design follows the humanist sans conventions of the broader Kohinoor system, prioritising legibility for body text applications. No italics or condensed cuts are included, keeping the family focused on straightforward typesetting needs.
foundry_blurb: Kohinoor Kannada is part of ITF's multi-script Kohinoor family, available in five styles from Light to Bold and sold individually or as a complete five-font package.

name: Begum Sans
classification_notes: Begum Sans is a Latin sans-serif with clear humanist proportions, related to the broader Begum family which spans Latin, Devanagari and Tamil scripts, suggesting a design approach rooted in multi-script harmony.
summary: A five-weight Latin sans-serif belonging to the wider Begum family, which extends across Latin, Devanagari and Tamil scripts.
description: Begum Sans offers five weights from Light to Bold, designed as the Latin member of a broader multi-script type system that includes Devanagari and Tamil siblings. The family's cross-script scope gives it a grounding in South Asian typographic traditions, which informs its proportions and spacing. Suitable for editorial and interface work where a clean sans with a broader cultural context is preferred.
foundry_blurb: Begum Sans is the Latin sans-serif companion to ITF's Begum family, which also includes Begum Devanagari and Begum Tamil. The complete Begum Sans family comprises five fonts spanning Light to Bold.

name: Diodrum Rounded
classification_notes: A rounded variant of Diodrum, a geometric sans-serif; the terminal rounding places it in the rounded geometric sans subcategory, retaining the clean, constructed letterforms of the base family.
summary: A rounded geometric sans with six weights and matching italics, extending the Diodrum family with softened terminals.
description: Diodrum Rounded applies rounded stroke terminals to the geometric structure of the original Diodrum, producing a warmer, more approachable character while retaining the same underlying proportions. The family runs across six weights from Extralight to Bold, each accompanied by an italic, giving twelve styles in total. Related family members include Diodrum with Greek and Cyrillic extensions, making it a practical choice for multilingual projects where a softer geometric sans is needed.
foundry_blurb: Diodrum Rounded is offered by Indian Type Foundry as a 12-font family available in six weights with matching italics, priced individually or as a complete collection. It sits alongside the core Diodrum family and its Greek and Cyrillic variants.

name: Diodrum Condensed
classification_notes: Diodrum Condensed follows the geometric sans-serif model, with clean, rational letterforms typical of the genre, presented here in a condensed width across multiple weights.
summary: A condensed geometric sans with six weights and matching italics, serving as the narrow companion to ITF's Diodrum family.
description: Twelve styles span six weights from Extralight to Bold, each with a matching italic, giving the family solid coverage for space-constrained layouts and editorial work. The condensed proportions make it well suited to headlines, navigation and narrow columns where a geometric sans is needed without sacrificing legibility. It pairs directly with the standard-width Diodrum for typographic systems requiring both registers.
foundry_blurb: Indian Type Foundry offer Diodrum Condensed as a 12-font family across six weights with italics, positioned as a condensed companion to the Diodrum typeface. The complete family is available as a bundle or as individual styles.

name: Hedra
classification_notes: The clean, regular letterforms and minimal stroke contrast visible in the specimen point to a geometric sans-serif construction, consistent with ITF's classification approach for this style.
summary: A five-weight geometric sans from Indian Type Foundry, spanning ExtraLight to Bold without italics or condensed cuts.
description: A compact geometric sans family available in five weights from ExtraLight to Bold. The range covers everyday text and mid-weight display needs, though the absence of italics and condensed variants limits its flexibility for extended typographic systems. Sold individually or as a complete five-font package.
foundry_blurb: ITF offer Hedra as a five-style geometric sans family, available as individual weights or as a complete bundle of all five fonts.

name: Troy Sans
classification_notes: Named 'Troy Sans' and offered as a sans-serif by ITF; minimal page detail available but the naming convention and classification context suggest a standard grotesque or humanist sans design.
summary: A single-weight sans-serif from Indian Type Foundry with very limited available style information.
description: Troy Sans is a sans-serif offered by Indian Type Foundry in a single Regular weight. The page provides little contextual detail about its design origins or intended use cases. At one style and a modest price point, it appears to be a restrained, workhorse-leaning typeface.
foundry_blurb: Indian Type Foundry offer Troy Sans as a single-font family, available as a Regular weight for purchase individually or as a complete set.

name: Troy
classification_notes: The page offers limited classification detail, but the name and single-weight presentation suggest a traditional serif design; categorised as transitional serif based on available visual context from the ITF page.
summary: A single-weight serif from Indian Type Foundry, offered as a standalone Regular cut.
description: Troy is a serif typeface from Indian Type Foundry available in a single Regular weight. The page provides minimal classification or designer information, positioning it as a straightforward text typeface. At just one style, it suits projects where a clean, unadorned serif is needed without a full family.
foundry_blurb: Indian Type Foundry offer Troy as a single-font family, available for purchase as a complete package at one weight.

name: Associate Slab Stencil
classification_notes: A stencil variant of a slab serif family, combining the structured serifs of a slab with the cut-through counters characteristic of stencil typefaces. Part of a wider Associate family that includes sans, mono and slab variants.
summary: A stencil cut of the Associate Slab family, running five weights from Extralight to Bold each with matching italics.
description: Part of the broader Associate type system alongside Associate Sans, Associate Sans Stencil, Associate Mono and Associate Slab, this stencil variant applies interrupted counters to a structured slab serif skeleton. Ten styles span five weights from Extralight to Bold with corresponding italics, making it usable across a range of display and branding contexts. The stencil treatment suits industrial, military-adjacent or modular design work where a harder edge is needed.
foundry_blurb: Associate Slab Stencil is part of Indian Type Foundry's Associate family, a suite of related typefaces including Associate Sans, Associate Sans Stencil, Associate Mono and Associate Slab. The complete family of ten fonts is available as a bundle.

name: Associate Slab
classification_notes: A slab serif with five weights and matching italics, part of a wider Associate family that includes sans, mono and stencil variants, indicating a purposeful workhorse slab design.
summary: A five-weight slab serif from Indian Type Foundry, part of a wider Associate superfamily that also covers sans, mono and stencil variants.
description: Ten styles span Extralight through Bold with matching italics, making it a practical slab for text and editorial work. It sits within the broader Associate family alongside Associate Sans, Associate Mono and Associate Sans Stencil, suggesting a coherent design system built for versatile deployment. The range covers standard Latin and is sold per style or as a complete ten-font bundle.
foundry_blurb: Associate Slab is part of Indian Type Foundry's Associate family, which includes Associate Sans, Associate Mono, Associate Sans Stencil and Associate Slab Stencil. The slab variant is offered in five weights from Extralight to Bold, each with a matching italic, available individually or as a complete ten-font package.
